name: Matthew Reynolds
text:
SENIOR GRAPHIC DESIGNER

Summary
Diverse, results-driven graphic designer with more than 30 years of experience in print, logo design, and dimensional signs. Passionate about comprehensive, strategic, and brand-building design. Proven ability to work independently and also collaborate within large design teams. Flexible, big-picture, out-of-the-box thinker and expert brainstormer, with a strong emphasis on conceptual design.

Highlights
Graphic Design Expertise
Creative Design Aptitude
Quality Assurance
Team Leadership
Adobe CC Suite
Strategic Planning
Training and Development
Corporate Design

Accomplishments
Successfully coordinated complex projects from the idea stage through design, mock-up, and final rendering to meet customer expectations while building customer loyalty.
Expanded client base through strategic relationship building and consistently delivering successful campaigns and designs.

Experience
03/2017 to 08/2018
Senior Graphic Designer
Company Name – City, State
Sketched designs for large home builders, such as Stanley Martin, Ryan Homes, NV Homes, Van Metre Homes, Stylecraft Homes, Dan Ryan Homes etc.
Followed strict branding guidelines when preparing sketches for client review.
Designed logos, interior acrylic display signs with stand off wall mounts, 3-D signs, impact logo signs, Routed Top signs, whip flags, mailboxes, take one boxes, feather flags, amenity signs, site ID sign, model ID signs, banners, and more!
Prepared all final print files.
Uploaded sketches and final print file links to Keyed IN initially, and then to Pace Software.
Cut vinyl on FC7500 Plotter.

01/2005 to 01/2017
Senior Graphic Designer/Production Manager
Company Name – City, State
Developed creative graphics that simplified complex messages.
Recommended techniques, methods and media best suited to produce desired visual effects.
Adhered to all corporate brand guidelines when preparing graphic materials.
Maximized operational efficiency by mentoring staff on various customer service initiatives.
Spearheaded and coordinated graphic design projects from conception to completion.
Collaborated with clients to create vision, conceive designs, and meet tight deadlines daily.
Developed excellent relationships with multiple vendors and outside installers to ensure the success of current and future projects.
Created and fashioned highly persuasive sales and marketing proposals.
Processed Estimates, Price Proposals & Invoices and e-mailed customers via Quick Books.
Printed in-house designs and customer prepared files on wide format printer.

05/1995 to 01/2005
Graphic Designer
Company Name – City, State
Designed interior and exterior signs, banners, trade show graphics, association graphics, dimensional signs, etc
Vectorized and designed client logos
Cut vinyl on Ioline Graphics Plotter
Printed graphics on HP 5500 large format printer

Education
Liberal Arts
Florida State University Panama Canal Branch
Visual Communications
Art Institute of Pittsburgh – City, State
Associates Degree

Skills
Invoicing: Quick Books
Software: Adobe Illustrator CC & CS6, Adobe Photoshop CC, Corel Draw x7, Microsoft Office Suite, Omega Composer
Printers: HP 5500, HP DesignJet L25500
Vinyl Plotters: Summa D120R, Graphtec, Ioline, HS15, FC 7500
Sign Tracking Software: Keyed IN and Pace

Portfolio
www.dmohanco.com
Linked In
https://www.linkedin.com/in/denyse-mohancographicartist

name: Eric Thompson
text:
WEBSITE DESIGNER         Summary     Software developer well-versed in the entire workflow for developing and implementing websites, including: consulting with the customer, designing mock-ups, programming the site, and incorporating customer feedback.       Skills          Visual Studio.Net  ASP, ASP.Net  MVC 4.0  Visual Basic.Net  C#  Microsoft SQL Server, SQL, Stored Procedures  TFS (Team Foundation Server)  HTML5, CSS3  Javascript, JQuery  AngularJS  LINQ  PHP, mySQL  Adobe Design Suite  Agile Software Development Methodology              Accomplishments      JCH Digital Designs Portfolio, which lists a number of client sites:
	http://www.cocs.com/jchdesigns/portfolio.htm.        Experience      Website Designer  ,   12/2016   to   Current    Company Name   －   City  ,   State      Currently working as a freelancer to redesign websites for my own clients and customers.  The process involved working with the client during all stages of development:.  Discussed requirements, documentation, and business needs regarding the design of the site.  Created mockups to get client approval before starting the coding.  Worked with the client throughout the testing stages to make sure they were happy.  Delivered the final website within the client's budget and the quoted timeframe.  Used: C#, ASP.Net, HTML5, CSS3, Javascript, JQuery, mySQL, Adobe Design Suite.          Senior Software Developer  ,   07/2016   to   11/2016    Company Name   －   City  ,   State      Contracted to Fiserv and assigned to the Velocity Loan Software Migration project which involved upgrading credit union clients from version 2 to 3.  This involved taking a client's XSLT and XML files and transforming them with custom mapping software into LUXML files, which were then processed by the Velocity software.  Our team reduced the number of hours it took to migrate a client by 62.5%, from 800 hours, down to 500 hours.  Worked with the lead developer to create testing and review processes to ensure the version 3 mapping files contained the same data and calculations as the version 2 mapping files.  Helped develop unit testing software to ensure the LUXML files were fully tested before delivering them to the client.  Supervised and reviewed the work of 3 offshore developers in India.  Used: XML, XSLT, Visual Basic, SQL Server, TFS, JIRA.          Support Engineer Tier III  ,   02/2016   to   04/2016    Company Name   －   City  ,   State      Contracted to Abila, a company which provides accounting and membership-management software to non-profit associations.  Investigated and resolved tickets escalated from the Tier II engineers.  Helped resolve the maintenance tickets in queue which resulted in improved metrics for our department and faster issue-resolution time for our customers.  Used C#, Visual Studio 2014, SQL Server 2012, TFS, Agile development, and .NET Framework 4.5.          Lead Software Developer  ,   12/2014   to   02/2016    Company Name   －   City  ,   State      A company which creates digital models for clients for use in artwork, animation, and other projects, as well as designs and develops websites.  Supervised and led a team of developers on a number of projects.  This included guiding and mentoring the junior developers and helping them improve their skills.  Worked as a project manager on a project which involved creating a model of a soccer player for a client.  This included going back and forth between the client and the model-maker to refine the model as needed.  Created, redesigned, and cleaned up websites for a number of clients, using C#, ASP.Net, AngularJS, and PHP.  Created digital models of a hospital interior for a medical equipment company.  This involved having phone meetings with the client to create a list of requirements which were passed to the model-maker.          Software Developer  ,   08/2014   to   12/2014    Company Name   －   City  ,   State      Assigned to Bank of America's CaseCura application, which gathers evidence and documents from for use in legal cases.  Created new user interface (UI) designs by replacing obsolete pages with data-bound dynamic grid web controls.  This improved the user experience, which led to happier users.  Led design and documentation sessions to get user feedback at every step in the design process.  This helped our team determine the best course of action for new feature requests and let the users be more involved in the decision-making process.  Led user-acceptance review meetings after the code changes were complete.  This ensured that the code met the user's specifications before the code was put into production.  Participated in daily scrum meetings to make sure all software development was on schedule.  Used: C#, MVC, TFS, .NET Framework 4.5, JIRA for ticket tracking, SQL Server, Agile development methodologies.          Software Developer  ,   10/2013   to   02/2014    Company Name   －   City  ,   State      Contracted to AgriLogic, a crop-insurance company.  Assigned to the AgAdvantage Legacy application, which processed crop insurance policies.  Also investigated and determined the best course of action for maintenance tickets received from the business analysts.  Worked closely with the business analysts and QA team to ensure all code followed the company's standards.  Was instrumental in reducing the number of maintenance tickets in the queue: when I started in October, there were around 50 tickets; by February, we managed to reduce the number to about 3 or 4 a day.  Participated in the weekly "Lunch & Learn" meetings where all the developers would discuss a current issue and brainstorm about how to solve it.  Used VB.Net, Visual Studio 2012, MVC, SQL Server, Atlassian JIRA (source code repository), .NET Framework 4.5.          Programmer Analyst  ,   02/2013   to   09/2013    Company Name   －   City  ,   State      FBC Mortgage is an Orlando based mortgage company.  Responsible for creating web-based applications to help increase the productivity of different departments, including:.  Created web-based reports for the underwriters to better help them determine which loans and customers needed their immediate attention.  Created a console-based application for the legal/ compliance department to alert them of any loans which could be considered high-risk so they could take appropriate action.  Created web-based reports for the CFO which analyzed the credit scores and debt-to-income ratio for loan applications so he could determine the financial risk of the loans.  Maintained and upgraded the existing web-based reports based on feedback from the loan openers, loan officers, and loan processors.  Helped to create and develop the Customer Mortgage Application, which let customers apply for a mortgage online.  I designed the graphics and layout, and then worked with the rest of team to connect the UI to the business layer of code.  Helped mentor and assist the junior-level developers with coding or design issues.  Used: Visual Studio 2012, ASP.Net, C#, MVC, Javascript, jQuery, Entity Framework 5, TFS (Team Foundation Server), Adobe Photoshop, web forms, SQL Server.          Lead Software Developer  ,   09/2004   to   02/2013    Company Name   －   City  ,   State      A company which creates digital models for clients for use in artwork, animation, and other projects, as well as designs and develops websites.  Supervised and led a team of developers on a number of projects.  This included guiding and mentoring the junior developers and helping them improve their skills.  Consulted and developed websites for clients, using HTML ASP, and C#, as well as mySQL for database access.  Managed a team of vendors who created their own digital models and products for sale at the Vanishing Point website.  I designed, created, and programmed the Vanishing Point website from scratch, using ASP and mySQL, which includes a marketplace to purchase and download products, an image gallery, and sales report pages for vendors.          Software Developer  ,   02/2002   to   09/2004    Company Name   －   City  ,   State      Contracted to AT&T in Orlando, FL as a member of the development team which upgraded, maintained, and monitored the Remittance Resolution System (RRS)- a system which processed over 8.7 million records and $4.2 billion in customer payments and refunds (2002 data).  My specific responsibilities included:
Monitored the daily reports to make sure they accurately reflected the payments processed by the system.  Upgraded the reports as needed, based on user feedback and requests.  Used VB 6, Windows NT, SQL Server 7, and Crystal Reports 8.5.  Re-designed and re-architected the RRS trouble-ticket database system to upgrade it from a Microsoft Access file to a completely new ASP/ web based application with a SQL Server database backend.  I was also assigned the task of adding additional features, such as an all-new interface (including new graphics and all-new layout), new security (users would now have to login), user auditing (tracking who updated what information), additional reports, additional fields (to accommodate Sarbanes-Oxley code-change regulations) and to consolidate numerous systems into one database.  Used ASP, HTML, and SQL Server 2000.  Hosted an hour-long panel discussion on the history and development of video games and digital artwork at MegaCon 2015 (April 2015).  Around 250 people attended, with people standing against the side and rear walls.  I've been using the Adobe Suite of tools since 1999.  I'm as comfortable with graphic design as I am with coding so I can wear multiple hats as part of a development team. Lead Software Developer, 12/2014 to 02/2016
Company Name - City, State
A company that creates digital models for clients for use in artwork, animation, and other projects, and also designs and develops websites. Software Developer, 08/2014 to 12/2014
Company Name - City, State
Assigned to Bank of America's CaseCura application, which gathers evidence and documents for use in legal cases. Created new user interface (UI) designs by replacing obsolete pages with data-bound dynamic grid web controls. This improved the user experience, resulting in happier users. Led design and documentation sessions to gather user feedback at every step of the design process. This helped the team determine the best approach for new feature requests and allowed users to be more involved in decision-making. Led user-acceptance review meetings after code changes were complete. This ensured the code met user specifications before being put into production. Participated in daily scrum meetings to ensure all software development stayed on schedule. Used: C#, MVC, TFS, .NET Framework 4.5, JIRA for ticket tracking, SQL Server, Agile development methodologies.

Software Developer, 10/2013 to 02/2014
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to AgriLogic, a crop-insurance company. Assigned to the AgAdvantage Legacy application, which processed crop insurance policies. Also investigated and determined the best course of action for maintenance tickets received from business analysts. Worked closely with the business analysts and QA team to ensure all code followed company standards. Was instrumental in reducing the number of maintenance tickets in the queue: when I started in October, there were around 50 tickets; by February, we reduced the number to about 3 or 4 a day. Participated in weekly "Lunch & Learn" meetings where all the developers discussed a current issue and brainstormed how to solve it. Used VB.Net, Visual Studio 2012, MVC, SQL Server, Atlassian JIRA (source code repository), .NET Framework 4.5.

Programmer Analyst, 02/2013 to 09/2013
Company Name - City, State
FBC Mortgage is an Orlando based mortgage company. Responsible for creating web-based applications to help increase productivity in different departments, including: Created web-based reports for underwriters to better help them determine which loans and customers needed immediate attention. Created a console-based application for the legal/compliance department to alert them to any loans that could be considered high-risk so they could take appropriate action. Created web-based reports for the CFO that analyzed credit scores and debt-to-income ratio for loan applications so he could determine the financial risk of the loans. Maintained and upgraded the existing web-based reports based on feedback from loan openers, loan officers, and loan processors. Helped create and develop the Customer Mortgage Application, which allowed customers to apply for a mortgage online. I designed the graphics and layout, then worked with the rest of the team to connect the UI to the business layer of code. Helped mentor and assist junior-level developers with coding or design issues. Used: Visual Studio 2012, ASP.Net, C#, MVC, Javascript, jQuery, Entity Framework 5, TFS (Team Foundation Server), Adobe Photoshop, web forms, SQL Server.

Lead Software Developer, 09/2004 to 02/2013
Company Name - City, State
A company that creates digital models for clients for use in artwork, animation, and other projects, and also designs and develops websites. Supervised and led a team of developers on several projects. This included guiding and mentoring junior developers and helping them improve their skills. Consulted on and developed websites for clients using HTML ASP and C#, as well as mySQL for database access. Managed a team of vendors who created their own digital models and products for sale at the Vanishing Point website. I designed, created, and programmed the Vanishing Point website from scratch using ASP and mySQL, which includes a marketplace to purchase and download products, an image gallery, and sales report pages for vendors.

Software Developer, 02/2002 to 09/2004
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to AT&T in Orlando, FL as part of the development team that upgraded, maintained, and monitored the Remittance Resolution System (RRS), a system that processed over 8.7 million records and $4.2 billion in customer payments and refunds (2002 data). My specific responsibilities included: Monitored daily reports to ensure they accurately reflected the payments processed by the system. Upgraded the reports as needed based on user feedback and requests. Used VB 6, Windows NT, SQL Server 7, and Crystal Reports 8.5. Re-designed and re-architected the RRS trouble-ticket database system to upgrade it from a Microsoft Access file to an entirely new ASP/web based application with a SQL Server database backend. I was also assigned to add additional features, such as a completely new interface (including new graphics and a new layout), new security (users would now have to login), user auditing (tracking who updated what information), additional reports, additional fields (to accommodate Sarbanes-Oxley code-change regulations), and to consolidate numerous systems into one database. Used ASP, HTML, and SQL Server 2000. Hosted an hour-long panel discussion on the history and development of video games and digital artwork at MegaCon 2015 (April 2015). Around 250 people attended, with people standing against the side and rear walls. I've been using the Adobe Suite of tools since 1999. I'm as comfortable with graphic design as I am with coding, so I can wear multiple hats as part of a development team.

University of Maryland - City, State

Monitored the daily reports to make sure they accurately reflected the payments processed by the system. Upgraded the reports as needed, based on user feedback and requests. Used VB 6, Windows NT, SQL Server 7, and Crystal Reports 8.5. Re-designed and re-architected the RRS trouble-ticket database system to upgrade it from a Microsoft Access file to a completely new ASP/web based application with a SQL Server database backend. I was also assigned the task of adding additional features, such as an all-new interface (including new graphics and all-new layout), new security (users would now have to login), user auditing (tracking who updated what information), additional reports, additional fields (to accommodate Sarbanes-Oxley code-change regulations) and to consolidate numerous systems into one database. Used ASP, HTML, and SQL Server 2000. Hosted an hour-long panel discussion on the history and development of video games and digital artwork at MegaCon 2015 (April 2015). Around 250 people attended, with people standing against the side and rear walls. I've been using the Adobe Suite of tools since 1999. I'm as comfortable with graphic design as I am with coding so I can wear multiple hats as part of a development team.

Education and Training
B.A : 1995
University of Maryland - City, State

Company Name - City, State
Currently employed as a freelancer redesigning websites for my own clients and customers. The process involved working with the client throughout all phases of development. Senior Software Developer, 07/2016 to 11/2016
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to Fiserv and assigned to the Velocity Loan Software Migration project, which entailed upgrading credit union clients from version 2 to 3. This involved taking a client's XSLT and XML files and converting them with custom mapping software into LUXML files, which were then processed by the Velocity software. Our team lowered the number of hours it took to migrate a client by 62.5%, from 800 hours to 500 hours. Worked with the lead developer to establish testing and review processes to ensure the version 3 mapping files contained the same data and calculations as the version 2 mapping files. Helped develop unit testing software to ensure the LUXML files were fully tested before being delivered to the client. Supervised and reviewed the work of 3 offshore developers in India. Used: XML, XSLT, Visual Basic, SQL Server, TFS, JIRA.

Support Engineer Tier III, 02/2016 to 04/2016
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to Abila, a company providing accounting and membership-management software to non-profit associations. Investigated and resolved tickets escalated from the Tier II engineers. Used C#, Visual Studio 2014, SQL Server 2012, TFS, Agile development, and .NET Framework 4.5.

Lead Software Developer, 12/2014 to 02/2016
Company Name - City, State
A company that creates digital models for clients for use in artwork, animation, and other projects, and also designs and develops websites. Supervised and led a team of developers on multiple projects. This included guiding and mentoring the junior developers and helping them improve their skills. Served as a project manager on a project involving the creation of a model of a soccer player for a client. This included going back and forth between the client and the model-maker to refine the model as needed. Created, redesigned, and cleaned up websites for a number of clients, using C#, ASP.Net, AngularJS, and PHP. Created digital models of a hospital interior for a medical equipment company. This involved phone meetings with the client to create a list of requirements which were passed to the model-maker.

Software Developer, 08/2014 to 12/2014
Company Name - City, State
Assigned to Bank of America's CaseCura application, which gathers evidence and documents for use in legal cases. Created new user interface (UI) designs by replacing obsolete pages with data-bound dynamic grid web controls. This improved the user experience, leading to happier users. Led design and documentation sessions to gather user feedback at every stage of the design process. This helped our team decide the best course of action for new feature requests and allowed users to be more involved in decision-making. Led user-acceptance review meetings after the code changes were complete. This ensured the code met the user's specifications before being put into production. Participated in daily scrum meetings to ensure all software development stayed on schedule. Used: C#, MVC, TFS, .NET Framework 4.5, JIRA for ticket tracking, SQL Server, Agile development methodologies.

Software Developer, 10/2013 to 02/2014
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to AgriLogic, a crop-insurance company. Assigned to the AgAdvantage Legacy application, which processed crop insurance policies. Also investigated and determined the best course of action for maintenance tickets received from the business analysts. Worked closely with the business analysts and QA team to ensure all code followed the company's standards. Was instrumental in reducing the number of maintenance tickets in the queue: when I started in October, there were around 50 tickets; by February, we reduced the number to about 3 or 4 a day. Participated in the weekly "Lunch & Learn" meetings where all the developers would discuss a current issue and brainstorm about how to solve it. Used VB.Net, Visual Studio 2012, MVC, SQL Server, Atlassian JIRA (source code repository), .NET Framework 4.5.

Programmer Analyst, 02/2013 to 09/2013
Company Name - City, State
FBC Mortgage is an Orlando based mortgage company. Responsible for creating web-based applications to help increase the productivity of different departments, including:. Created web-based reports for the underwriters to better help them determine which loans and customers needed their immediate attention. Created a console-based application for the legal/ compliance department to alert them of any loans which could be considered high-risk so they could take appropriate action. Created web-based reports for the CFO which analyzed the credit scores and debt-to-income ratio for loan applications so he could determine the financial risk of the loans. Maintained and upgraded the existing web-based reports based on feedback from the loan openers, loan officers, and loan processors. Helped to create and develop the Customer Mortgage Application, which let customers apply for a mortgage online. I designed the graphics and layout, and then worked with the rest of team to connect the UI to the business layer of code. Helped mentor and assist the junior-level developers with coding or design issues. Lead Software Developer, 12/2014 to 02/2016
Company Name - City, State
A company that creates digital models for clients for use in artwork, animation, and other projects, and also designs and develops websites. Worked as a project manager on a project that involved creating a model of a soccer player for a client. Software Developer, 08/2014 to 12/2014
Company Name - City, State

Led design and documentation sessions to gather user feedback at every step of the design process. This helped the team determine the best approach for new feature requests and allowed users to be more involved in decision-making. Led user-acceptance review meetings after code changes were complete. This ensured the code met user specifications before being put into production. Software Developer, 10/2013 to 02/2014
Company Name - City, State

Worked closely with the business analysts and QA team to ensure all code followed company standards. Was instrumental in reducing the number of maintenance tickets in the queue: when I started in October, there were around 50 tickets; by February, we reduced the number to about 3 or 4 a day. Participated in weekly "Lunch & Learn" meetings where all the developers discussed a current issue and brainstormed how to solve it. Used VB.Net, Visual Studio 2012, MVC, SQL Server, Atlassian JIRA (source code repository), .NET Framework 4.5.

Programmer Analyst, 02/2013 to 09/2013
Company Name - City, State
FBC Mortgage is an Orlando based mortgage company. Responsible for creating web-based applications to help increase productivity in different departments, including: Created web-based reports for underwriters to better help them determine which loans and customers needed immediate attention. Created a console-based application for the legal/compliance department to alert them to any loans that could be considered high-risk so they could take appropriate action. Created web-based reports for the CFO that analyzed credit scores and debt-to-income ratio for loan applications so he could determine the financial risk of the loans. Maintained and upgraded the existing web-based reports based on feedback from loan openers, loan officers, and loan processors. Helped create and develop the Customer Mortgage Application, which allowed customers to apply for a mortgage online. I designed the graphics and layout, then worked with the rest of the team to connect the UI to the business layer of code. Helped mentor and assist junior-level developers with coding or design issues. Used: Visual Studio 2012, ASP.Net, C#, MVC, Javascript, jQuery, Entity Framework 5, TFS (Team Foundation Server), Adobe Photoshop, web forms, SQL Server.

Lead Software Developer, 09/2004 to 02/2013
Company Name - City, State
A company that creates digital models for clients for use in artwork, animation, and other projects, and also designs and develops websites. Software Developer, 02/2002 to 09/2004
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to AT&T in Orlando, FL as part of the development team that upgraded, maintained, and monitored the Remittance Resolution System (RRS), a system that processed over 8.7 million records and $4.2 billion in customer payments and refunds (2002 data). My specific responsibilities included: Monitored daily reports to ensure they accurately reflected the payments processed by the system. Upgraded the reports as needed based on user feedback and requests. Used VB 6, Windows NT, SQL Server 7, and Crystal Reports 8.5. I was also assigned to add additional features, such as a completely new interface (including new graphics and a new layout), new security (users would now have to login), user auditing (tracking who updated what information), additional reports, additional fields (to accommodate Sarbanes-Oxley code-change regulations), and to consolidate numerous systems into one database. Used ASP, HTML, and SQL Server 2000. Hosted an hour-long panel discussion on the history and development of video games and digital artwork at MegaCon 2015 (April 2015). Around 250 people attended, with people standing against the side and rear walls. I've been using the Adobe Suite of tools since 1999. I'm as comfortable with graphic design as I am with coding, so I can wear multiple hats as part of a development team.

Around 250 people attended, with people standing against the side and rear walls. I've been using the Adobe Suite of tools since 1999. I'm as comfortable with graphic design as I am with coding so I can wear multiple hats as part of a development team.

Company Name - City, State

Programmer Analyst, 02/2013 to 09/2013
Company Name - City, State
FBC Mortgage is an Orlando based mortgage company. Responsible for creating web-based applications to help increase the productivity of different departments, including:. Created web-based reports for the underwriters to better help them determine which loans and customers needed their immediate attention. Created web-based reports for the CFO which analyzed the credit scores and debt-to-income ratio for loan applications so he could determine the financial risk of the loans. Maintained and upgraded the existing web-based reports based on feedback from the loan openers, loan officers, and loan processors. Helped to create and develop the Customer Mortgage Application, which let customers apply for a mortgage online. I designed the graphics and layout, and then worked with the rest of team to connect the UI to the business layer of code. Software Developer, 02/2002 to 09/2004
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to AT&T in Orlando, FL as a member of the development team that upgraded, maintained, and monitored the Remittance Resolution System (RRS)- a system which processed over 8.7 million records and $4.2 billion in customer payments and refunds (2002 data). My specific responsibilities included:
Monitored the daily reports to ensure they accurately reflected the payments processed by the system. Upgraded the reports as needed, based on user feedback and requests. Used VB 6, Windows NT, SQL Server 7, and Crystal Reports 8.5. Software Developer, 02/2002 to 09/2004
Company Name - City, State

Senior Software Developer, 07/2016 to 11/2016
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to Fiserv and assigned to the Velocity Loan Software Migration project, which consisted of upgrading credit union clients from version 2 to 3. This required taking a client's XSLT and XML files and converting them with custom mapping software into LUXML files, which were then processed by the Velocity software. Our team decreased the time needed to migrate a client by 62.5%, from 800 hours to 500 hours. Collaborated with the lead developer to build testing and review processes to ensure the version 3 mapping files held the same data and calculations as the version 2 mapping files. Assisted in developing unit testing software to ensure the LUXML files were fully tested before being delivered to the client. Supervised and reviewed the work of 3 offshore developers in India. Used: XML, XSLT, Visual Basic, SQL Server, TFS, JIRA.

Support Engineer Tier III, 02/2016 to 04/2016
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to Abila, a company that supplies accounting and membership-management software to non-profit associations. Investigated and resolved tickets escalated from the Tier II engineers. Used C#, Visual Studio 2014, SQL Server 2012, TFS, Agile development, and .NET Framework 4.5.

Lead Software Developer, 12/2014 to 02/2016
Company Name - City, State
A company that develops digital models for clients for use in artwork, animation, and other projects, and also designs and develops websites. Supervised and led a team of developers on numerous projects. This included guiding and mentoring junior developers and assisting them in improving their skills. Served as a project manager on a project that involved creating a model of a soccer player for a client. This included going back and forth between the client and the model-maker to refine the model as needed. Created, redesigned, and cleaned up websites for several clients, using C#, ASP.Net, AngularJS, and PHP. Created digital models of a hospital interior for a medical equipment company. This involved phone meetings with the client to create a list of requirements that were passed to the model-maker.

Software Developer, 08/2014 to 12/2014
Company Name - City, State
Assigned to Bank of America's CaseCura application, which collects evidence and documents for use in legal cases. Created new user interface (UI) designs by replacing outdated pages with data-bound dynamic grid web controls. This improved the user experience, which resulted in happier users. Led design and documentation sessions to obtain user feedback at each step of the design process. This helped our team determine the best path for new feature requests and allowed users to be more involved in decision-making. Led user-acceptance review meetings once the code changes were finished. This ensured the code met the user's specifications before it went into production. Participated in daily scrum meetings to ensure all software development remained on schedule. Used: C#, MVC, TFS, .NET Framework 4.5, JIRA for ticket tracking, SQL Server, Agile development methodologies.

Software Developer, 10/2013 to 02/2014
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to AgriLogic, a crop-insurance company. Assigned to the AgAdvantage Legacy application, which handled crop insurance policies. Worked closely with the business analysts and QA team to ensure all code met the company's standards. Played an important role in reducing the number of maintenance tickets in the queue: when I started in October, there were around 50 tickets; by February, we reduced the number to about 3 or 4 a day. Participated in the weekly "Lunch & Learn" meetings where all the developers discussed a current issue and brainstormed how to solve it. Used VB.Net, Visual Studio 2012, MVC, SQL Server, Atlassian JIRA (source code repository), .NET Framework 4.5.

Company Name - City, State

Created web-based reports for underwriters to better help them determine which loans and customers needed their immediate attention. Created a console-based application for the legal/compliance department to alert them to any loans that could be considered high-risk so they could take appropriate action. Created web-based reports for the CFO that analyzed the credit scores and debt-to-income ratio for loan applications so he could determine the financial risk of the loans. Maintained and upgraded the existing web-based reports based on feedback from the loan openers, loan officers, and loan processors. Helped create and develop the Customer Mortgage Application, which let customers apply for a mortgage online. I designed the graphics and layout, and then worked with the rest of the team to connect the UI to the business layer of code. name: Michael Nguyen
text:
WEBSITE DESIGNER

Summary
Software developer experienced with the full workflow for building and implementing websites, including consulting with the customer, creating mock-ups, programming the site, and integrating customer feedback.

Skills
Visual Studio.Net, ASP, ASP.Net, MVC 4.0, Visual Basic.Net, C#, Microsoft SQL Server, SQL, Stored Procedures, TFS (Team Foundation Server), HTML5, CSS3, Javascript, JQuery, AngularJS, LINQ, PHP, mySQL, Adobe Design Suite, Agile Software Development Methodology

Accomplishments
JCH Digital Designs Portfolio, which lists a number of client sites:
http://www.cocs.com/jchdesigns/portfolio.htm.

Experience
Website Designer, 12/2016 to Current
Company Name - City, State
Currently working as a freelancer redesigning websites for my own clients and customers. The process included working with the client through all stages of development. Discussed requirements, documentation, and business needs related to the site's design. Created mockups to obtain client approval before beginning coding. Worked with the client during testing to ensure they were satisfied. Delivered the final website within the client's budget and the quoted timeframe. Used: C#, ASP.Net, HTML5, CSS3, Javascript, JQuery, mySQL, Adobe Design Suite.

Senior Software Developer, 07/2016 to 11/2016
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to Fiserv and assigned to the Velocity Loan Software Migration project, which involved upgrading credit union clients from version 2 to 3. This required taking a client's XSLT and XML files and converting them with custom mapping software into LUXML files, which were then processed by the Velocity software. Our team cut the number of hours required to migrate a client by 62.5%, from 800 hours down to 500 hours. Worked with the lead developer to build testing and review processes to ensure the version 3 mapping files had the same data and calculations as the version 2 mapping files. Helped develop unit testing software to ensure the LUXML files were fully tested before delivery to the client. Supervised and reviewed the work of 3 offshore developers in India. Used: XML, XSLT, Visual Basic, SQL Server, TFS, JIRA.

Support Engineer Tier III, 02/2016 to 04/2016
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to Abila, a company that provides accounting and membership-management software to non-profit associations. Investigated and resolved tickets escalated from the Tier II engineers. Helped resolve maintenance tickets in queue, which improved department metrics and sped up issue resolution for customers. Used C#, Visual Studio 2014, SQL Server 2012, TFS, Agile development, and .NET Framework 4.5.

Lead Software Developer, 12/2014 to 02/2016
Company Name - City, State
A company that creates digital models for clients for use in artwork, animation, and other projects, and also designs and develops websites. Supervised and led a team of developers on several projects. This included guiding and mentoring junior developers and helping them build their skills. Worked as a project manager on a project that involved creating a model of a soccer player for a client. This required going back and forth between the client and the model-maker to refine the model as needed. Created, redesigned, and cleaned up websites for several clients using C#, ASP.Net, AngularJS, and PHP. Created digital models of a hospital interior for a medical equipment company. This involved phone meetings with the client to create a list of requirements that were passed to the model-maker.

Software Developer, 08/2014 to 12/2014
Company Name - City, State
Assigned to Bank of America's CaseCura application, which gathers evidence and documents for use in legal cases. Created new user interface (UI) designs by replacing obsolete pages with data-bound dynamic grid web controls. This improved the user experience, resulting in happier users. Led design and documentation sessions to gather user feedback at every step of the design process. This helped the team determine the best approach for new feature requests and allowed users to be more involved in decision-making. Led user-acceptance review meetings after code changes were complete. This ensured the code met user specifications before being put into production. Participated in daily scrum meetings to ensure all software development stayed on schedule. Used: C#, MVC, TFS, .NET Framework 4.5, JIRA for ticket tracking, SQL Server, Agile development methodologies.

Software Developer, 10/2013 to 02/2014
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to AgriLogic, a crop-insurance company. Assigned to the AgAdvantage Legacy application, which processed crop insurance policies. Also investigated and determined the best course of action for maintenance tickets received from business analysts. Worked closely with the business analysts and QA team to ensure all code followed company standards. Was instrumental in reducing the number of maintenance tickets in the queue: when I started in October, there were around 50 tickets; by February, we reduced the number to about 3 or 4 a day. Participated in weekly "Lunch & Learn" meetings where all the developers discussed a current issue and brainstormed how to solve it. Used VB.Net, Visual Studio 2012, MVC, SQL Server, Atlassian JIRA (source code repository), .NET Framework 4.5.

Programmer Analyst, 02/2013 to 09/2013
Company Name - City, State
FBC Mortgage is an Orlando based mortgage company. Responsible for creating web-based applications to help increase productivity in different departments, including: Created web-based reports for underwriters to better help them determine which loans and customers needed immediate attention. Created a console-based application for the legal/compliance department to alert them to any loans that could be considered high-risk so they could take appropriate action. Created web-based reports for the CFO that analyzed credit scores and debt-to-income ratio for loan applications so he could determine the financial risk of the loans. Maintained and upgraded the existing web-based reports based on feedback from loan openers, loan officers, and loan processors. Helped create and develop the Customer Mortgage Application, which allowed customers to apply for a mortgage online. I designed the graphics and layout, then worked with the rest of the team to connect the UI to the business layer of code. Helped mentor and assist junior-level developers with coding or design issues. Used: Visual Studio 2012, ASP.Net, C#, MVC, Javascript, jQuery, Entity Framework 5, TFS (Team Foundation Server), Adobe Photoshop, web forms, SQL Server.

Lead Software Developer, 09/2004 to 02/2013
Company Name - City, State
A company that creates digital models for clients for use in artwork, animation, and other projects, and also designs and develops websites. Supervised and led a team of developers on several projects. This included guiding and mentoring junior developers and helping them improve their skills. Consulted on and developed websites for clients using HTML ASP and C#, as well as mySQL for database access. Managed a team of vendors who created their own digital models and products for sale at the Vanishing Point website. I designed, created, and programmed the Vanishing Point website from scratch using ASP and mySQL, which includes a marketplace to purchase and download products, an image gallery, and sales report pages for vendors.

Software Developer, 02/2002 to 09/2004
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to AT&T in Orlando, FL as part of the development team that upgraded, maintained, and monitored the Remittance Resolution System (RRS), a system that processed over 8.7 million records and $4.2 billion in customer payments and refunds (2002 data). My specific responsibilities included: Monitored daily reports to ensure they accurately reflected the payments processed by the system. Upgraded the reports as needed based on user feedback and requests. Used VB 6, Windows NT, SQL Server 7, and Crystal Reports 8.5. Re-designed and re-architected the RRS trouble-ticket database system to upgrade it from a Microsoft Access file to an entirely new ASP/web based application with a SQL Server database backend. I was also assigned to add additional features, such as a completely new interface (including new graphics and a new layout), new security (users would now have to login), user auditing (tracking who updated what information), additional reports, additional fields (to accommodate Sarbanes-Oxley code-change regulations), and to consolidate numerous systems into one database. Used ASP, HTML, and SQL Server 2000. Hosted an hour-long panel discussion on the history and development of video games and digital artwork at MegaCon 2015 (April 2015). Around 250 people attended, with people standing against the side and rear walls. I've been using the Adobe Suite of tools since 1999. I'm as comfortable with graphic design as I am with coding, so I can wear multiple hats as part of a development team.

Education and Training
B.A : 1995
University of Maryland - City, State

Software Developer, 02/2002 to 09/2004
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to AT&T in Orlando, FL as a member of the development team that upgraded, maintained, and monitored the Remittance Resolution System (RRS)- a system which processed over 8.7 million records and $4.2 billion in customer payments and refunds (2002 data). Used: C#, ASP.Net, HTML5, CSS3, Javascript, JQuery, mySQL, Adobe Design Suite.

Senior Software Developer, 07/2016 to 11/2016
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to Fiserv and assigned to the Velocity Loan Software Migration project, which involved upgrading credit union clients from version 2 to 3. This involved taking a client's XSLT and XML files and transforming them with custom mapping software into LUXML files, which were then processed by the Velocity software. Our team reduced the time it took to migrate a client by 62.5%, from 800 hours to 500 hours. Worked with the lead developer to create testing and review processes to ensure the version 3 mapping files held the same data and calculations as the version 2 mapping files. Helped develop unit testing software to ensure the LUXML files were fully tested before delivery to the client. Supervised and reviewed the work of 3 offshore developers in India. Used: XML, XSLT, Visual Basic, SQL Server, TFS, JIRA.

Support Engineer Tier III, 02/2016 to 04/2016
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to Abila, a company which provides accounting and membership-management software to non-profit associations. Investigated and resolved tickets escalated from the Tier II engineers. Used C#, Visual Studio 2014, SQL Server 2012, TFS, Agile development, and .NET Framework 4.5.

Lead Software Developer, 12/2014 to 02/2016
Company Name - City, State
A company that creates digital models for clients for use in artwork, animation, and other projects, and also designs and develops websites. Supervised and led a team of developers on a number of projects. This included guiding and mentoring junior developers and helping them improve their skills. Worked as a project manager on a project involving the creation of a model of a soccer player for a client. This included going back and forth between the client and the model-maker to refine the model as needed. Created, redesigned, and cleaned up websites for a number of clients, using C#, ASP.Net, AngularJS, and PHP. Created digital models of a hospital interior for a medical equipment company. This involved phone meetings with the client to create a list of requirements that were passed to the model-maker.

Software Developer, 08/2014 to 12/2014
Company Name - City, State
Assigned to Bank of America's CaseCura application, which gathers evidence and documents for use in legal cases. Created new user interface (UI) designs by replacing obsolete pages with data-bound dynamic grid web controls. This improved the user experience, which led to happier users. Led design and documentation sessions to get user feedback at every step of the design process. This helped our team determine the best course of action for new feature requests and let users be more involved in decision-making. Led user-acceptance review meetings after the code changes were complete. This ensured the code met the user's specifications before it was put into production. Participated in daily scrum meetings to make sure all software development was on schedule. Used: C#, MVC, TFS, .NET Framework 4.5, JIRA for ticket tracking, SQL Server, Agile development methodologies.

Software Developer, 10/2013 to 02/2014
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to AgriLogic, a crop-insurance company. Assigned to the AgAdvantage Legacy application, which processed crop insurance policies. Also investigated and determined the best course of action for maintenance tickets received from the business analysts. Worked closely with the business analysts and QA team to ensure all code followed the company's standards. Was instrumental in reducing the number of maintenance tickets in the queue: when I started in October, there were around 50 tickets; by February, we managed to reduce the number to about 3 or 4 a day. Participated in the weekly "Lunch & Learn" meetings where all the developers would discuss a current issue and brainstorm about how to solve it. Used VB.Net, Visual Studio 2012, MVC, SQL Server, Atlassian JIRA (source code repository), .NET Framework 4.5.

Programmer Analyst, 02/2013 to 09/2013
Company Name - City, State

Created web-based reports for the underwriters to better help them determine which loans and customers needed their immediate attention. Created a console-based application for the legal/ compliance department to alert them of any loans which could be considered high-risk so they could take appropriate action. Created web-based reports for the CFO which analyzed the credit scores and debt-to-income ratio for loan applications so he could determine the financial risk of the loans. Maintained and upgraded the existing web-based reports based on feedback from the loan openers, loan officers, and loan processors. Helped to create and develop the Customer Mortgage Application, which let customers apply for a mortgage online. I designed the graphics and layout, and then worked with the rest of team to connect the UI to the business layer of code. Company Name - City, State

Software Developer, 08/2014 to 12/2014
Company Name - City, State

Led user-acceptance review meetings once the code changes were finished. This ensured the code met the user's specifications before it went into production. Participated in daily scrum meetings to ensure all software development remained on schedule. Used: C#, MVC, TFS, .NET Framework 4.5, JIRA for ticket tracking, SQL Server, Agile development methodologies.

Software Developer, 10/2013 to 02/2014

Programmer Analyst, 02/2013 to 09/2013
Company Name - City, State

Created web-based reports for underwriters to better help them determine which loans and customers needed their immediate attention. Created web-based reports for the CFO that analyzed the credit scores and debt-to-income ratio for loan applications so he could determine the financial risk of the loans. Maintained and upgraded the existing web-based reports based on feedback from the loan openers, loan officers, and loan processors. Helped create and develop the Customer Mortgage Application, which let customers apply for a mortgage online. I designed the graphics and layout, and then worked with the rest of the team to connect the UI to the business layer of code. text:

Summary
Software developer experienced with the full workflow for building and implementing websites, including consulting with the customer, creating mock-ups, programming the site, and integrating customer feedback.

Skills

Accomplishments
JCH Digital Designs Portfolio, which lists a number of client sites:
http://www.cocs.com/jchdesigns/portfolio.htm.

Experience
Website Designer, 12/2016 to Current
Company Name - City, State
Currently working as a freelancer redesigning websites for my own clients and customers. The process included working with the client through all stages of development. Discussed requirements, documentation, and business needs related to the site's design. Created mockups to obtain client approval before beginning coding. Worked with the client during testing to ensure they were satisfied. Delivered the final website within the client's budget and the quoted timeframe. Used: C#, ASP.Net, HTML5, CSS3, Javascript, JQuery, mySQL, Adobe Design Suite.

Senior Software Developer, 07/2016 to 11/2016
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to Fiserv and assigned to the Velocity Loan Software Migration project, which involved upgrading credit union clients from version 2 to 3. This required taking a client's XSLT and XML files and converting them with custom mapping software into LUXML files, which were then processed by the Velocity software. Our team cut the number of hours required to migrate a client by 62.5%, from 800 hours down to 500 hours. Worked with the lead developer to build testing and review processes to ensure the version 3 mapping files had the same data and calculations as the version 2 mapping files. Helped develop unit testing software to ensure the LUXML files were fully tested before delivery to the client. Supervised and reviewed the work of 3 offshore developers in India. Used: XML, XSLT, Visual Basic, SQL Server, TFS, JIRA.

Support Engineer Tier III, 02/2016 to 04/2016
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to Abila, a company that provides accounting and membership-management software to non-profit associations. Investigated and resolved tickets escalated from the Tier II engineers. Helped resolve maintenance tickets in queue, which improved department metrics and sped up issue resolution for customers. Used C#, Visual Studio 2014, SQL Server 2012, TFS, Agile development, and .NET Framework 4.5.

Lead Software Developer, 12/2014 to 02/2016
Company Name - City, State
A company that creates digital models for clients for use in artwork, animation, and other projects, and also designs and develops websites. Supervised and led a team of developers on several projects. This included guiding and mentoring junior developers and helping them build their skills. Worked as a project manager on a project that involved creating a model of a soccer player for a client. This required going back and forth between the client and the model-maker to refine the model as needed. Created, redesigned, and cleaned up websites for several clients using C#, ASP.Net, AngularJS, and PHP. Created digital models of a hospital interior for a medical equipment company. This involved phone meetings with the client to create a list of requirements that were passed to the model-maker.

Software Developer, 08/2014 to 12/2014
Company Name - City, State
Assigned to Bank of America's CaseCura application, which gathers evidence and documents for use in legal cases. Created new user interface (UI) designs by replacing obsolete pages with data-bound dynamic grid web controls. This improved the user experience, resulting in happier users. Led design and documentation sessions to gather user feedback at every step of the design process. This helped the team determine the best approach for new feature requests and allowed users to be more involved in decision-making. Led user-acceptance review meetings after code changes were complete. This ensured the code met user specifications before being put into production. Participated in daily scrum meetings to ensure all software development stayed on schedule. Used: C#, MVC, TFS, .NET Framework 4.5, JIRA for ticket tracking, SQL Server, Agile development methodologies.

Software Developer, 10/2013 to 02/2014
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to AgriLogic, a crop-insurance company. Assigned to the AgAdvantage Legacy application, which processed crop insurance policies. Also investigated and determined the best course of action for maintenance tickets received from business analysts. Worked closely with the business analysts and QA team to ensure all code followed company standards. Was instrumental in reducing the number of maintenance tickets in the queue: when I started in October, there were around 50 tickets; by February, we reduced the number to about 3 or 4 a day. Participated in weekly "Lunch & Learn" meetings where all the developers discussed a current issue and brainstormed how to solve it. Used VB.Net, Visual Studio 2012, MVC, SQL Server, Atlassian JIRA (source code repository), .NET Framework 4.5.

Programmer Analyst, 02/2013 to 09/2013
Company Name - City, State
FBC Mortgage is an Orlando based mortgage company. Responsible for creating web-based applications to help increase productivity in different departments, including: Created web-based reports for underwriters to better help them determine which loans and customers needed immediate attention. Created a console-based application for the legal/compliance department to alert them to any loans that could be considered high-risk so they could take appropriate action. Created web-based reports for the CFO that analyzed credit scores and debt-to-income ratio for loan applications so he could determine the financial risk of the loans. Maintained and upgraded the existing web-based reports based on feedback from loan openers, loan officers, and loan processors. Helped create and develop the Customer Mortgage Application, which allowed customers to apply for a mortgage online. I designed the graphics and layout, then worked with the rest of the team to connect the UI to the business layer of code. Helped mentor and assist junior-level developers with coding or design issues. Used: Visual Studio 2012, ASP.Net, C#, MVC, Javascript, jQuery, Entity Framework 5, TFS (Team Foundation Server), Adobe Photoshop, web forms, SQL Server.

Lead Software Developer, 09/2004 to 02/2013
Company Name - City, State
A company that creates digital models for clients for use in artwork, animation, and other projects, and also designs and develops websites. Supervised and led a team of developers on several projects. This included guiding and mentoring junior developers and helping them improve their skills. Consulted on and developed websites for clients using HTML ASP and C#, as well as mySQL for database access. Managed a team of vendors who created their own digital models and products for sale at the Vanishing Point website. I designed, created, and programmed the Vanishing Point website from scratch using ASP and mySQL, which includes a marketplace to purchase and download products, an image gallery, and sales report pages for vendors.

Software Developer, 02/2002 to 09/2004
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to AT&T in Orlando, FL as part of the development team that upgraded, maintained, and monitored the Remittance Resolution System (RRS), a system that processed over 8.7 million records and $4.2 billion in customer payments and refunds (2002 data). My specific responsibilities included: Monitored daily reports to ensure they accurately reflected the payments processed by the system. Upgraded the reports as needed based on user feedback and requests. Used VB 6, Windows NT, SQL Server 7, and Crystal Reports 8.5. Re-designed and re-architected the RRS trouble-ticket database system to upgrade it from a Microsoft Access file to an entirely new ASP/web based application with a SQL Server database backend. I was also assigned to add additional features, such as a completely new interface (including new graphics and a new layout), new security (users would now have to login), user auditing (tracking who updated what information), additional reports, additional fields (to accommodate Sarbanes-Oxley code-change regulations), and to consolidate numerous systems into one database. Used ASP, HTML, and SQL Server 2000. Hosted an hour-long panel discussion on the history and development of video games and digital artwork at MegaCon 2015 (April 2015). Around 250 people attended, with people standing against the side and rear walls. I've been using the Adobe Suite of tools since 1999. I'm as comfortable with graphic design as I am with coding, so I can wear multiple hats as part of a development team.

Education and Training
B.A : 1995
University of Maryland - City, State

Helped develop unit testing software to ensure the LUXML files were fully tested before delivery to the client. Supervised and reviewed the work of 3 offshore developers in India. Used: XML, XSLT, Visual Basic, SQL Server, TFS, JIRA.

Support Engineer Tier III, 02/2016 to 04/2016
Company Name - City, State

Lead Software Developer, 12/2014 to 02/2016
Company Name - City, State

Software Developer, 08/2014 to 12/2014
Company Name - City, State
Assigned to Bank of America's CaseCura application, which gathers evidence and documents for use in legal cases. This helped our team determine the best course of action for new feature requests and let users be more involved in decision-making. Used: C#, MVC, TFS, .NET Framework 4.5, JIRA for ticket tracking, SQL Server, Agile development methodologies.

Software Developer, 10/2013 to 02/2014
Company Name - City, State

Worked closely with the business analysts and QA team to ensure all code followed the company's standards. Programmer Analyst, 02/2013 to 09/2013
Company Name - City, State
FBC Mortgage is an Orlando based mortgage company. Responsible for creating web-based applications to help increase the productivity of different departments, including:. Created web-based reports for the underwriters to better help them determine which loans and customers needed their immediate attention. Created web-based reports for the CFO which analyzed the credit scores and debt-to-income ratio for loan applications so he could determine the financial risk of the loans. Maintained and upgraded the existing web-based reports based on feedback from the loan openers, loan officers, and loan processors. Helped to create and develop the Customer Mortgage Application, which let customers apply for a mortgage online. I designed the graphics and layout, and then worked with the rest of team to connect the UI to the business layer of code. Helped mentor and assist the junior-level developers with coding or design issues. Used: Visual Studio 2012, ASP.Net, C#, MVC, Javascript, jQuery, Entity Framework 5, TFS (Team Foundation Server), Adobe Photoshop, web forms, SQL Server.

Lead Software Developer, 09/2004 to 02/2013
Company Name - City, State
A company which creates digital models for clients for use in artwork, animation, and other projects, as well as designs and develops websites. Company Name - City, State
Contracted to AT&T in Orlando, FL as a member of the development team that upgraded, maintained, and monitored the Remittance Resolution System (RRS)- a system which processed over 8.7 million records and $4.2 billion in customer payments and refunds (2002 data). Monitored the daily reports to make sure they accurately reflected the payments processed by the system. Upgraded the reports as needed, based on user feedback and requests. Around 250 people attended, with people standing against the side and rear walls. I've been using the Adobe Suite of tools since 1999. I'm as comfortable with graphic design as I am with coding so I can wear multiple hats as part of a development team.

Website Designer, 12/2016 to Current
Company Name - City, State
Currently employed as a freelancer redesigning websites for my own clients and customers. The process involved working with the client throughout all phases of development. Worked with the client through the testing stages to ensure satisfaction. Delivered the finished website within the client's budget and the quoted timeframe. Used: C#, ASP.Net, HTML5, CSS3, Javascript, JQuery, mySQL, Adobe Design Suite.

Senior Software Developer, 07/2016 to 11/2016
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to Fiserv and assigned to the Velocity Loan Software Migration project, which entailed upgrading credit union clients from version 2 to 3. This involved taking a client's XSLT and XML files and converting them with custom mapping software into LUXML files, which were then processed by the Velocity software. Our team lowered the number of hours it took to migrate a client by 62.5%, from 800 hours to 500 hours. Worked with the lead developer to establish testing and review processes to ensure the version 3 mapping files contained the same data and calculations as the version 2 mapping files. Helped develop unit testing software to ensure the LUXML files were fully tested before being delivered to the client. Supervised and reviewed the work of 3 offshore developers in India. Used: XML, XSLT, Visual Basic, SQL Server, TFS, JIRA.

Support Engineer Tier III, 02/2016 to 04/2016
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to Abila, a company providing accounting and membership-management software to non-profit associations. Investigated and resolved tickets escalated from the Tier II engineers. Helped resolve maintenance tickets in queue, resulting in improved metrics for our department and faster issue-resolution time for our customers. Used C#, Visual Studio 2014, SQL Server 2012, TFS, Agile development, and .NET Framework 4.5.

Lead Software Developer, 12/2014 to 02/2016
Company Name - City, State
A company that creates digital models for clients for use in artwork, animation, and other projects, and also designs and develops websites. Supervised and led a team of developers on multiple projects. This included guiding and mentoring the junior developers and helping them improve their skills. Served as a project manager on a project involving the creation of a model of a soccer player for a client. This included going back and forth between the client and the model-maker to refine the model as needed. Created, redesigned, and cleaned up websites for a number of clients, using C#, ASP.Net, AngularJS, and PHP. Created digital models of a hospital interior for a medical equipment company. This involved phone meetings with the client to create a list of requirements which were passed to the model-maker.

Software Developer, 08/2014 to 12/2014
Company Name - City, State
Assigned to Bank of America's CaseCura application, which gathers evidence and documents for use in legal cases. Created new user interface (UI) designs by replacing obsolete pages with data-bound dynamic grid web controls. This improved the user experience, leading to happier users. Led design and documentation sessions to gather user feedback at every stage of the design process. This helped our team decide the best course of action for new feature requests and allowed users to be more involved in decision-making. Led user-acceptance review meetings after the code changes were complete. This ensured the code met the user's specifications before being put into production. Participated in daily scrum meetings to ensure all software development stayed on schedule. Used: C#, MVC, TFS, .NET Framework 4.5, JIRA for ticket tracking, SQL Server, Agile development methodologies.

Software Developer, 10/2013 to 02/2014
Company Name - City, State
Contracted to AgriLogic, a crop-insurance company. Assigned to the AgAdvantage Legacy application, which processed crop insurance policies. Also investigated and determined the best course of action for maintenance tickets received from the business analysts. Worked closely with the business analysts and QA team to ensure all code followed the company's standards. Was instrumental in reducing the number of maintenance tickets in the queue: when I started in October, there were around 50 tickets; by February, we reduced the number to about 3 or 4 a day. Participated in the weekly "Lunch & Learn" meetings where all the developers would discuss a current issue and brainstorm about how to solve it. Used VB.Net, Visual Studio 2012, MVC, SQL Server, Atlassian JIRA (source code repository), .NET Framework 4.5.

Company Name - City, State

Created a console-based application for the legal/ compliance department to alert them of any loans which could be considered high-risk so they could take appropriate action. Created web-based reports for the CFO which analyzed the credit scores and debt-to-income ratio for loan applications so he could determine the financial risk of the loans. Maintained and upgraded the existing web-based reports based on feedback from the loan openers, loan officers, and loan processors. Helped to create and develop the Customer Mortgage Application, which let customers apply for a mortgage online. I designed the graphics and layout, and then worked with the rest of team to connect the UI to the business layer of code. Helped mentor and assist the junior-level developers with coding or design issues. name: Brian Sullivan
text:
SR. TOOLING DESIGNER

Professional Profile
Experienced Mechanical Designer who creates high-quality CAD models and engineering drawings across a range of technical manufacturing industries, with experience in manufacturing and advanced technology industries. Background includes designing, detailing, assembling, debugging, and using a strong machine shop background. Design and drafting experience includes equipment for tooling, fixtures, automated machines, structural steel detailing, sheet metal design, Engineering Change Order and Red Line drawing changes, weldment fixtures, and hands-on fabrication. Autodesk Inventor experience spans release 10 through 2015, and SolidWorks experience spans release 97 Plus, certified, trained in 2008-2009, with current work experience with 2010. Drawing standards are compliant with ANSI/ASME, ANSI/AWS, as well as ITAR, with a good understanding of both English and Metric Geometric Dimensioning and Tolerancing Techniques.

Qualifications
Inventor modeling; drafting techniques; creating bills of materials; detail-oriented; interpersonal skills; negotiation skills.

Experience
Sr. Tooling Designer, 01/2010 to Current
Company Name, City, State
Used Autodesk Inventor 2012, 2015, and AutoCAD 2015 to create original tooling designs and working drawings. Supported domestic and foreign manufacturing facilities in troubleshooting tooling design issues. Processed Engineering change requests to prepare and update drawings to current standards. Member of the GD&T taskforce, working with a cross functional group to make drafting changes and standard for the Metal Packaging Division.

Mechanical Design Engineer, 01/2010 to 01/2010
Company Name, City, State
Processed drawing changes for the Ion Beam coating chambers on both standard and custom configuration chambers. Performed original design work to simplify and reduce the interior shielding in order to establish a standard for future configurations. Design and drawing software was SolidWorks.

Mechanical Design Engineer, 01/2010 to 01/2010
Company Name, City, State
Retrofitted existing tooling and designed additional features for the LeyBold coating chamber for production work. Created original part designs, solid models, detailing, Iges or Step files for solid model geometry, and PDF files for in-house data file management. Revised and updated electronic drawing files, providing hard copies of the latest revisions to production teams. Used standard methods to ensure work was checked back into the project's correct file location. Design and drawing software included SolidWorks.

Mechanical Design Engineer, 01/2010 to 01/2010
Company Name, City, State
Worked on design improvements for an existing environmentally controlled disk drive test chamber to prepare it for production. Processed engineering changes for 3D CAD solid model designs and corrections of existing solid models. Design and drawing software included Autodesk Inventor.

Mechanical Design Engineer, 01/2010 to 01/2010
Company Name, City, State
Created a new tooling assembly fixture design to improve production efficiency. Original design of two test prototypes and one working model. Fabricated, assembled, and demonstrated use of working prototype for proof of concept. Designed an iteration of the prototype for a higher-capacity assembly fixture to be used in high volume production. New part design, design reviews, solid modeling, 3D stereolithography file, detailing, Iges or Step files for solid models. Design and drawing software included SolidWorks.

Mechanical Design Engineer, 01/2009 to 01/2009
Company Name, City, State
Performed original design work on new parts and fixtures for Diode Array, as well as the BAK and MSP coating chambers. Supported and processed design reviews, solid models, and detailed drawings. Processed Iges or Step files for solid model geometry and created PDF files for in-house data file management. Worked on revisions to update the electronic drawing files and created hard copies for production use. Design and drawing software included SolidWorks 2009.

Mechanical Designer, 01/2008 to 01/2008
Company Name, City, State
Supported project work with the director of engineering to process engineering changes to the custom coating chambers for the Solar Panels production line. Worked on a tight schedule and deadlines for new part design, creating solid model changes, detailed drawings of weldments, and revisions and updates of electronic drawing files. Processed ECO paper work, printing out the latest revisions. Responsible for the original design of a new self-contained Solar Panel, custom mobile storage and transportation cart. Design and drawing software included Autodesk Inventor 2008 and Vault.

Senior Mechanical Designer, 01/2004 to 01/2008
Company Name, City, State
Designed and detailed custom coating fixtures and tooling for the BAK, LeyBold, and MSP coating chambers. Designed original parts, creating solid CAD models, detailed drawings, Iges or Step files for solid model geometry, and PDF files for in-house data file management into SAP. Processed revisions and updates for electronic drawing files, and created hard copies of the latest revisions for production use. Supported the machine shop manager as Assistant Manager for all in-house machining work. Backup operator and maintainer for 3D printing operations using Invision S2. Created CAD models and downloaded solid model parts to produce working 3D stereolithography rapid prototype parts for engineering test work and analysis. Aided with mechanical fixture and tooling equipment for assembly of rear projection light engines. Direct design assistant to the head BAK and LeyBold coating engineers for two and half years. Design and drawing software included Autodesk Inventor 10.

Education
Associate of Applied Science: Machine Drafting Technology, May 1992
Front Range Community College, City, State, United States
Machine Drafting Technology

Skills
3D, Assistant Manager, AutoCAD, Backup, CAD, concept, drafting, features, file management, functional, drawing, machining, director, mechanical, modeling, Packaging, PDF, process engineering, SAP, SolidWorks, transportation, trouble-shooting.

name: Brianna Davis
text:
SR. TOOLING DESIGNER

Professional Profile
Experienced Mechanical Designer who creates high-quality CAD models and engineering drawings across a range of technical manufacturing industries, with experience in manufacturing and advanced technology industries. Background includes designing, detailing, assembling, debugging, and using a strong machine shop background. Design and drafting experience includes equipment for tooling, fixtures, automated machines, structural steel detailing, sheet metal design, Engineering Change Order and Red Line drawing changes, weldment fixtures, and hands-on fabrication. Autodesk Inventor experience spans release 10 through 2015, and SolidWorks experience spans release 97 Plus, certified, trained in 2008-2009, with current work experience with 2010. Drawing standards are compliant with ANSI/ASME, ANSI/AWS, as well as ITAR, with a good understanding of both English and Metric Geometric Dimensioning and Tolerancing Techniques.

Qualifications
Inventor modeling; drafting techniques; creating bills of materials; detail-oriented; interpersonal skills; negotiation skills.
